responsibilities:
Deichmann Mobile Shopping Application:

  • Contributed to the development of a large-scale Android e-commerce platform supporting multiple white-label brands (e.g., Dosenbach and Ochsner Shoes in Switzerland, vanHaren in the Netherlands and in Belgium, and Solebox) through a shared codebase.
  • Implemented and maintained core e-commerce features including product catalog, search, checkout flows, store locator, and Google wallet integrations. Contributed to localization and multi-language support across multiple markets. Integrated and debugged analytics tracking (Firebase/Google Analytics), ensuring accurate event tracking and session behavior.
  • Contributing to the design and implementation of an SDK that decouples the application from backend services by providing an abstraction layer, handling API communication, and centralizing business logic.
  • Automated testing, build, and release processes using GitLab CI/CD, streamlining delivery pipelines and enabling reliable publishing to multiple target channels.
  • Worked in an Agile environment, continuously improving core user flows, code quality, performance, and application stability for an app with 5M+ downloads and a 4.7 rating on the Google Play Store.

Used methods and tools:
Android Studio, Kotlin, Java, Agile, MVVM, Room DB, Hilt DI, Retrofit, Coroutines, Flow, Arrow - Either, App Distribution, Crashlytics, Remote Config, Postman.

BitaTouch:

  • Developing an Android application that runs on Android TV and Chromebox OS. This is an Android app that runs on a large touch-screen like a Kiosk to let the user interact with it and place an order. This application has different build variants to support other companies besides Deichman like (Dosenbach and Ochsner Shoes in Switzerland, vanHaren in the Netherlands and in Belgium) by creating multiple applications with only one code-base.
  • Support a digital agency to create the Android client mobile app.
  • Create libraries to use in the app and provide it to the agency that develops the mobile app.
  • Develop a backend using .Net Core to let Mandates manage their advertisements that appear on the screens in each store.

Used methods and tools:
Android Studio, Visual Studio, Kotlin, Java, Agile, MVP, Kiosk mood, Google Maps, Room DB, Dagger Dependency Injection.

• Implement new features to PhotoEditor SDK and VideoEditor SDK in the Android version.
  • Create plugins for Cordova and Ionic to wrap the native PhotoEditor SDK and VideoEditor SDK to make it easily used and integrated with apps built using Cordova and Ionic without forcing the developers to leave their JavaScript tooling and programming environment.
tools: Android Studio, Visual Studio Code, Java, Kotlin, Cordova, Ionic, C++, OpenGl.

company:

responsibilities:
